findings.problem: Token surface "兵者" treats 兵 and 者 as a single token, but 者 is a separate grammatical particle that the grammar note explicitly addresses. The note "The particle 者 nominalizes the preceding noun" requires 者 to be its own token to align with the grammatical explanation. Single-character tokens are preferred per the schema rules; "兵者" is not a fixed compound or name that justifies a multi-character token.
findings.suggestedFix: Split into two tokens: surface "兵" (start 4, end 5) and surface "者" (start 5, end 6). Update glossIds and grammar note anchorTokenIds accordingly.
